title: tailor configuration
description:
Schema for ALL tailor configuration files. tailor wraps the Azure Linux Image Customizer (IC). There are three document kinds, each validated by a definition below:
  • tailor.yaml (workspace/tool config) -> #/$defs/ToolConfig
  • image.yaml  (an image definition)   -> #/$defs/ImageDefinition
  • by-*/<value>.yaml (a fragment)      -> #/$defs/Fragment
Point an editor at the file with a fragment, e.g. `# yaml-language-server: $schema=./tailor.schema.json#/$defs/ImageDefinition`.

IMPORTANT: the contents of an image's `config:` key are Image Customizer configuration and are INTENTIONALLY NOT modeled here — tailor treats them as an opaque YAML tree (an inline mapping) or a path string, merges them structurally, and passes them to IC via --config-file. Only the tailor surface is validated.

ToolConfig.title: tailor.yaml — workspace / tool config
ToolConfig.description: The workspace root. Holds repo-wide settings (toolchains, runtime, defaults) and the image catalogue. Found by walking UP from the cwd, Cargo-style.

schemaVersion.description: tailor manifest schema version. Only tailor.yaml / tailor.lock carry it.

ImageDefinition.title: image.yaml — an image definition
ImageDefinition.description: One buildable image (the base document). May declare a `matrix:` that expands into many cells; per-axis deltas live in sibling by-*/ fragment files. Top-level keys are tailor fields ONLY; all Image Customizer config lives under `config:`.

name.description: Unique image id; used in output filenames and on the CLI. No '_' — that is the reserved separator in output cell-slugs (see reference/types.md).

injectFiles.description: Run IC inject-files signing when the IC config sets output.artifacts. Default false.

extraDependencies.description: Extra input paths whose mtime/hash feed the incremental up-to-date check. The only way to track files referenced from INSIDE the (opaque) IC config (scripts, additionalFiles, overlays, repo files, ...).

Fragment.title: by-*/<value>.yaml — a fragment (partial image definition)
Fragment.description: A conditional delta merged onto the image's base document. Selected by its directory path (by-release/4.0.yaml applies when release==4.0) and/or an inline `match:`. Every field is optional, and a field's VALUE may be wrapped in a directive ($set/$replace/$remove/$include/$rename/$select). `$`-prefixed top-level keys (e.g. a whole-document $include) are also allowed.

^\\$.$comment: Permit top-level directives such as $include in a fragment.

Toolchains.description: Repo-wide Image Customizer toolchain(s). Each entry resolves according to its pull policy and lockfile state. Registry/RepoDigest results are pinned in tailor.lock; local Id-only images are not lockable.

default.description: Name of the entry in `entries` used by any image that omits `toolchain:`.

entries.description: List of named pinned IC containers. Each item carries a unique `name`.

ToolchainEntry.description: An Image Customizer container. `name` and `container` are required; the pull tag defaults to `tag`, else `version`, else `latest`. `pull` defaults to `missing`.

container.description: Registry path of the IC image, e.g. mcr.microsoft.com/azurelinux/imagecustomizer.

version.description: Optional, informational semantic version of IC. tailor does not gate IC versions. When `tag` is absent, this is used as the pull tag.

tag.description: Registry tag actually pulled. Defaults to `version` (MCR publishes unprefixed tags, e.g. :1.3.0), and to `latest` when neither `tag` nor `version` is set. The resolved DIGEST is authoritative and is written to tailor.lock.

ToolchainRef.description: Per-image toolchain selection. EITHER a name referencing tailor.yaml `toolchains.entries` (workspace mode), OR an inline ToolchainEntry (standalone mode, no tailor.yaml). Omit entirely to use the workspace default (or tailor's built-in default when standalone).

oneOf.description: Name of a toolchain defined in tailor.yaml.

PullPolicy.description: Container image pull policy. `always` resolves/pulls from a registry. `missing` uses a local image if present (locked digest wins), else resolves/pulls. `never` requires a locked digest or local image and never pulls. Local Id-only images are usable but not lockable.

ToolsDirSources.description: Named container root filesystems tailor exports, caches, and passes to IC as --tools-dir. Each item carries a unique name and resolves according to its pull policy.

ToolsDirSource.description: A named tools-dir source. `tag` defaults to `latest`; `pull` defaults to `missing`. Registry/RepoDigest results are pinned in tailor.lock when locked; local Id-only images are not lockable.

container.description: Container image whose flattened rootfs provides the tools dir.

tag.description: Registry tag. Defaults to latest when the container reference has no tag/digest.

ToolsDirSourceInline.description: Inline tools-dir source fields for image.yaml toolsDir.source.

container.description: Container image whose flattened rootfs provides the tools dir.

tag.description: Registry tag. Defaults to latest when the container reference has no tag/digest.

ToolsDir.description: Tailor-managed IC --tools-dir. Requires the inline IC config previewFeatures list to include tools-dir.

Runtime.description: Container-runtime (bollard/Docker) knobs reproducing IC's invocation contract. Sensible defaults; rarely overridden.

privileged.description: Default true; IC requires a privileged container.

hostRoot.description: Container-side namespace prefix for host->container path translation. Default /host. This is not a whole-host bind.

extraPaths.description: Additional host paths exposed under hostRoot. Read-only by default; writable only with access: rw. Relative paths resolve against the workspace root.

buildDirBase.description: Host filesystem base for per-cell IC --build-dir values (<buildDirBase>/<slug>). Must be on a separate filesystem from /. Omit to use container-local /tmp.

imageCacheDir.description: Host directory for IC's --image-cache-dir (caches base images downloaded from a registry). Resolved relative to the manifest, created on the host, passed host-translated. Needed only when an image uses an `oci`/`azureLinux` base; tailor may auto-default it (e.g. ./.tailor/cache) when a registry base is present.

janitorImage.description: Digest-pinned minimal image used for sudo-free ownership/cleanup of IC's root-owned outputs (design.md §7.7).

tag.description: Default 'latest'; resolved to a digest in tailor.lock.

ExtraMount.description: Explicit additional host path exposed under runtime.mounts.hostRoot.

path.description: Host path. Relative paths resolve against the workspace root.

MountAccess.description: Container bind access. Defaults to read-only.

Defaults.description: Defaults applied to every image that does not set the field itself.

ImageCatalogue.description: Which images belong to the workspace. OMIT the whole key to auto-discover every */image.yaml at depth 1 (the common case). Provide it to curate the set or inline trivial images.

members.description: Path/glob strings to member directories (each containing an image.yaml) or directly to image.yaml files, resolved relative to tailor.yaml. Default: ["*/"].

exclude.description: Path/glob strings to exclude from the discovered/listed members.

inline.description: Images defined directly inside tailor.yaml (for trivial single-file images that don't warrant their own directory).

BaseImageCatalogue.description: Named base image catalogue for image.yaml `base: { ref: <name> }` references. Each item carries a unique `name`. Use one entry per architecture for per-arch local files, selected from by-arch/<arch>.yaml fragments.

BaseImageEntry.description: A named base image file, optionally scoped to one architecture and annotated with its original upstream source.

name.description: Unique slot name within `baseImages`; referenced by image.yaml `base: { ref: <name> }`.

path.description: Local image file path resolved relative to tailor.yaml.

BaseImageSource.description: Optional provenance for a catalogue base image. Exactly one source kind.

uri.description: OCI reference the local file was derived from.

azureLinux.description: MCR Azure Linux source the local file was derived from.

Architectures.description: Target architectures (the arch matrix axis). Each is one IC invocation per output.

Arch.description: Architecture token. Maps to container platform linux/<arch>.

Matrix.description: The image's axis declaration AND build set: each user-defined axis maps to its CLOSED list of values; the cartesian product (minus `exclude`, plus `include`) is the set of cells built. `arch` may be given here as an axis or via `architectures`. Axis names match ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$ and exclude the reserved keys `include`/`exclude`.

include.description: Extra cells to add beyond the product.

exclude.description: Cells to remove from the product.

^(?!include$|exclude$)[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9.description: An axis: its closed set of values. Each value must match [A-Za-z0-9.-]+ (no '_', the reserved cell-slug separator) so it is safe as both a fragment filename and an output-name segment.

CellSelector.description: A partial cell: a map of axis name -> a single pinned value.

OutputsField.description: Output matrix for the image. A plain list of OutputSpec, or a list-directive ($replace/$remove/$include) when used in a fragment.

OutputSpec.description: One output format. Each output x architecture is one IC invocation -> one artifact.

cosiCompressionLevel.description: zstd level; only valid for format: cosi (requires IC >= 1.2).

name.description: Output-name template that overrides the default cell slug. Supports ${...} interpolation of axis values plus ${name}/${arch}/${format}. The DEFAULT basename is the full cell slug: <image-name>_<each declared axis value in matrix order>_<format>, '_'-separated (see reference/image-yaml.md). tailor computes every output path up front and rejects collisions.

OutputFormat.description: IC --output-image-format. The `convert` operation supports only a subset: vhd, vhd-fixed, vhdx, qcow2, raw, cosi, baremetal-image (iso / pxe-* are customize-only).

BaseField.description: The base image source for the image. A BaseSource, or a directive ($set/$select/$include) when used in a fragment to override an inherited base.

BaseSource.description: EXACTLY ONE base source. Drives IC's input image via the command line (overrides any input.image in the IC config).

path.description: Local image file (e.g. a .vhdx). Single-arch; for per-arch local files use the baseImages catalogue with one slot per arch.

ref.description: Name of an entry in tailor.yaml `baseImages` (a reference to a catalogue slot).

uri.description: OCI reference, e.g. registry/repo:tag (the resolved digest is pinned in tailor.lock).

platform.description: e.g. linux/amd64. Defaults to linux/<arch> per cell.

azureLinux.description: MCR Azure Linux sugar over `oci`. Multi-arch manifest -> one source covers every arch.

FeatureFlags.description: Image-level feature flags (e.g. pcrlock-static-files). Enable by-feature/<name>.yaml fragments and gate optional config. NOTE: this is NOT the design.md `features.operation`/`features.injectFiles` object — those operation flags are the top-level `operation` and `injectFiles` keys (renamed to avoid the collision).

Params.description: Named scalar constants for ${...} interpolation into config string values (and into other params/axis values). Values are scalars only — never structure.

RpmSourcesField.description: Extra RPM sources (each becomes an IC --rpm-source, lowest->highest priority). An entry is a path to a directory of RPMs OR a .repo file. A plain list, or a list-directive in a fragment.

Operation.description: tailor-level IC operation. Default customize. `convert` forbids `config`, requires a local-file base, forbids rpmSources/injectFiles, and restricts the output-format set.

OpaqueICConfig.description: Image Customizer --config-file content: EITHER an inline mapping (the IC config) OR a string path to an IC config YAML. OPAQUE to tailor — its inner structure is NOT modeled or validated by this schema; tailor merges it structurally (with directives) and passes it through to IC.

Match.description: A fragment condition (ANDed with the fragment's directory-path predicate). Supports axis equality {axis: value}, set membership {axis: [values]}, a {feature: name} test, and the boolean combinators all/any/not.

IncludeDirective.description: $include — splice the parsed content of a repo-root-relative file as this value (covers mappings, lists, and scalars). Sole key.

SetDirective.description: $set — explicit override of an inherited scalar or object value (resolves an otherwise-conflicting double-set).

ReplaceDirective.description: $replace — replace an inherited list wholesale (instead of the default append).

RemoveDirective.description: $remove — drop the listed items from an inherited list.

RenameDirective.description: $rename — rename a keyed list element and fix dependent references.

SelectDirective.description: $select — OPTIONAL co-location sugar: pick this value by an axis. { $select: { <axis>: { <value>: <result>, ..., default: <result> } } }. The primary mechanism is a per-axis file, not this.
